data.title: Nike's results were better than expected, but investors still aren't sold on its turnaround
data.description:
By Bill Peters

Nike's stock drops after CEO says progress was 'different' across various areas of the business

Nike reported quarterly results on Tuesday afternoon.

Nike on Tuesday reported quarterly results that were a bit better than Wall Street's expectations - but investors still didn't appear convinced of the sneaker giant's turnaround efforts, and its CEO said progress was "different" across various areas of the business.

Nike (NKE) is trying to recover from weaker demand in recent years, as higher prices for gasoline and other basics steer people away from sneaker purchases. The company's stock as of last week hovered at nine-year lows, and analysts have debated the state of its efforts to make more products that suit athletes' needs and push further into running shoes and women's apparel.

"The pace of progress is different across the portfolio and the areas we prioritized first continue to drive momentum," CEO Elliott Hill said in a statement on Tuesday.

Those remarks echoed comments Hill made during Nike's last round of earnings in December, when he said the recovery would likely be uneven across Nike's different products and geographies.

Shares of Nike were down 2.6% in after-hours trading Tuesday, erasing most of the 3.1% bounce seen during the regular session ahead of the results. On Monday, the stock had closed at its lowest price since October 2017.

Nike said that for its fiscal third quarter that ran through February, sales were flat from the previous year at $11.28 billion, as gains in North America offset continued struggles in China. The company earned 35 cents a share, down from 54 cents a share the prior year.

The average estimates among analysts polled by FactSet were for sales of $11.23 billion and earnings per share of 29 cents.

Sales for Nike's business with outside retailers rose 5% during the quarter. However, sales at its own physical and online stores fell 4%. Gross margin, a measure of the profitability of sales, fell to 40.2%, from 41.5%.

After trying to sell more products itself over recent years, Nike has tried to work more with retail chains to recharge sales growth. And after competition and discounting hurt online sales, the company has tried to turn its own stores and its e-commerce business into a destination for higher-end, full-priced products.

Under Hill's leadership, Nike has also tried to thin down its stockpiles of casual sneakers, which have seen popularity wane. But as it tries to make more room for more new products, UBS analysts recently said that data showed some of Nike's newer offerings weren't catching on.

The company's postearnings conference call with analysts is scheduled to start at 5 p.m. Eastern time.

Ahead of the call, analysts also had questions about the impact of the Iran war, Nike's business in the Middle East, its preparation for this year's FIFA World Cup and the Supreme Court's ruling striking down President Donald Trump's emergency-powers tariffs.

As of Tuesday's close, Nike's stock had dropped 16.8% over the past 12 months, while the S&P 500 index SPX had gained 16.3%.

-Bill Peters

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Nike Continues to Face Challenges From China, Tariffs — Market Talk
data.description: Nike's fiscal third-quarter results were in line with the company's expectations, CFO Matthew Friend says, and came in above Wall Street's expectations, according to FactSet. However, weak demand in China and added tariff costs continue to hurt the sneaker and athletic apparel company's results. Sales in China fell 7% in the quarter, and higher tariffs in North America squeezed margins, the company says. Shares fall 3.1%, to $51.16, in after-hours trading. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: International Seaways Adds Guarantor and VLCC Collateral to $500 Million Revolving Credit Facility
data.description:
International Seaways entered into a joinder agreement to its existing $500 million revolving credit facility. Subsidiary Hendricks Tanker Company joined as a guarantor and pledged a VLCC tanker as collateral, replacing previously released assets in the collateral pool. The move is intended to support the company’s liquidity and maintain borrowing capacity under the facility.

  • Counterparty: Nordea Bank, New York Branch, as administrative agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 27 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Co-terminous with existing facility
  • Reason: Enhance collateral coverage and maintain borrowing capacity

Original SEC Filing: International Seaways, Inc. [ INSW ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

data.title: Catheter Precision reports 95% revenue gain in 2025, narrows net loss to $17.2M
data.description:
Catheter Precision reported 2025 revenue of $819,000, up 95% from $420,000 in 2024, with a gross margin of 92.3%. The company recorded a 2025 net loss of $17.2 million (including $7.8 million of non-cash items) and completed post-year-end strategic moves including the acquisition of Flyte and financing to strengthen its balance sheet. Management said the business will operate two distinct platforms — medical devices and regional aviation — with multiple near-term commercial and strategic catalysts.

Financial Highlights

  • 2025 revenue: $819 thousand, a 95% increase versus 2024 revenue of $420 thousand.
  • 2025 gross margin: 92.3% (compared with 90.0% in 2024).
  • 2025 net loss: $17.2 million, of which $7.8 million was non-cash per company reconciliation.
  • Q4 2025 revenue: $238 thousand, a 60% increase from Q4 2024 revenue of $148 thousand.
  • Q4 2025 net loss: $5.3 million, of which $3.5 million was non-cash per company reconciliation.

Business Highlights

  • Received CE Mark for LockeT in April 2025 and completed first commercial LockeT sales in Europe.
  • Expanded customer base with 32 new customers adopting VIVO and LockeT platforms during 2025.
  • Advanced clinical visibility with four abstracts accepted at the Heart Rhythm Society meeting and ongoing clinical validation of VIVO and LockeT.
  • Acquired Flyte (announced post-year-end) to establish a second operating segment: a technology-enabled regional air mobility platform with active revenue-generating operations and a scalable Cirrus Vision Jet fleet.
  • Completed financing initiatives after year-end to strengthen capital resources to support multi-segment growth and expansion plans.

Original SEC Filing: Catheter Precision, Inc. [ VTAK ] - 8-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Lion Copper & Gold 10-K: Net income $4.38M, EPS $0.04
data.description:
Lion Copper & Gold reported net income of $4.383 million for the year, driven primarily by a $26.381 million non‑cash gain on the deconsolidation of Falcon Copper. The company recorded an operating loss of $16.660 million driven by general and administrative expenses and share‑based compensation, and reported basic earnings per share of $0.04 and diluted EPS of $0.03.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $4.383 million, reflecting a $26.381 million non‑cash gain on deconsolidation of Falcon Copper.
  • Net income and comprehensive income (loss) for the year: $4.383 million, impacted by the deconsolidation gain and other non‑cash items.
  • Earnings (loss) per share, basic: $0.04 (net income attributable to shareholders cited at $14.771 million before noncontrolling interest adjustments).
  • Earnings (loss) per share, diluted: $0.03 (diluted weighted shares: 484.301 million).
  • Operating loss: $(16.660) million, driven by general and administrative expenses of $10.066 million and share‑based compensation of $8.772 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trends and MD&A focus: Management discusses year‑over‑year top‑line trends and the drivers expected to affect 2025 results.
  • Channel shifts: Management highlights a strategic movement toward higher digital and direct‑to‑consumer sales channels and reduced reliance on third‑party distributors.
  • Brand momentum: Emphasis on strengthened brand positioning, improved marketing return on investment, and higher customer retention metrics.
  • Operational initiatives: Execution of productivity improvements, supply‑chain enhancements, and scaling of key production and fulfillment capacities were noted as operational priorities.

Original SEC Filing: LION COPPER & GOLD CORP. [ LCGMF ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Interactive Strength reports $11.5M revenue, $(23.97)M net loss in 2025 10-K
data.description:
Interactive Strength reported consolidated revenue of $11.53 million for the fiscal year, driven largely by the July 2025 Wattbike acquisition, while net loss narrowed to $(23.968) million compared with $(34.934) million in 2024. Gross profit recovered to $0.913 million with an 8% gross margin, and the company noted improved operating results though it remained loss-making at the operating level. EPS was not reported explicitly in the filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $11.53 million, a 114% increase versus $5.38 million in 2024, driven by acquisitions.
  • Gross profit: $0.913 million (gross margin 8%), improved from a $(2.78) million loss in 2024.
  • Loss from operations: $(19.858) million, improved from $(29.185) million in 2024.
  • Net loss: $(23.968) million, improved from $(34.934) million in 2024.
  • Net income per share: Metric not reported explicitly and therefore omitted.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth and M&A: Consolidated revenue rose 114% year-over-year to $11.5 million in 2025, driven primarily by the July 2025 acquisition of Wattbike and resulting three-brand portfolio including CLMBR and Ergatta.
  • Channel and product mix: A shift toward commercial hardware sales increased the fitness product mix to roughly 90% of revenue, which lowered subscription attach rates.
  • Operational efficiency: Gross profit improvement was attributed to Wattbike contribution and reduced R&D spending, helping margin recovery compared with the prior year loss.
  • Expansion strategy: Focus on scaling via distributor partnerships, international expansion, B2B/light-commercial channels, and vertical partnerships to broaden market reach and drive future growth.

Original SEC Filing: Interactive Strength, Inc. [ TRNR ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Greenlane Holdings 10-K: Revenue $4.36M, EPS (11.42) on $85.6M Net Loss
data.description:
Greenlane Holdings reported fiscal 2025 net revenue of $4.355 million and a net loss per Class A common share of $(11.42), reflecting a sharp decline in legacy sales and significant non-cash and advisory charges that widened the loss to $(85.580) million. Revenue fell about 67% year-over-year as the company scaled down warehouse inventory sales while transitioning its business model. The results were also driven by an inventory impairment and unrealized digital asset losses that materially depressed gross and net profitability.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenue: $4.355 million, down $8.920 million (67%) versus 2024.
  • Gross (loss) profit: $(12.465) million, compared with $6.282 million in 2024, driven in part by a $6.3 million inventory impairment.
  • Loss from operations: $(54.246) million; operating expenses increased to $41.781 million, which include $18.553 million related to advisory warrants.
  • Net loss: $(85.580) million, compared with $(17.657) million in 2024, including $31.147 million of unrealized losses on digital assets.
  • Net loss attributable to Class A common stock per share: $(11.42) on weighted-average shares of 7.492 million.

Business Highlights

  • Strategic pivot to digital treasury: Completed in October 2025, Greenlane shifted from a distribution model to a BERA-focused digital asset treasury strategy.
  • Revenue and channel shift: The company scaled down legacy warehouse sales and moved commerce toward a drop-ship, asset-light model, contributing to the revenue decline.
  • Operational streamlining: Exited warehouse inventory, adopted a drop-ship approach, and reduced operating headcount to align with the new business model.
  • BERA integration and participation: Established a treasury policy, implemented staking/Proof-of-Liquidity (PoL) activities, governance participation, and formed a Digital Assets Committee to oversee the strategy.
  • Liquidity and operations support: A private placement provided cash, stablecoins, and BERA tokens to fund the digital treasury strategy while supporting remaining legacy operations.

Original SEC Filing: Greenlane Holdings, Inc. [ GNLN ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Cantor Equity Partners VI 10-K: Net loss $64K in 2025, $4K in 2024
data.description:
Cantor Equity Partners VI reported net losses of approximately $64,000 for the year ended December 31, 2025 and $4,000 for the year ended December 31, 2024; revenue and per-share figures were not reported in the filing. The company completed an initial public offering in February 2026, raising $115 million into a trust to fund a future business combination and related activities. Sponsor commitments for pre- and post-IPO funding and advisory support were disclosed to support diligence, marketing and transaction costs as it pursues a target business combination.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income (Loss) — Year ended Dec 31, 2025: Net loss of approximately $0.064 million (about $64,000).
  • Net Income (Loss) — Year ended Dec 31, 2024: Net loss of approximately $0.004 million (about $4,000).
  • Net Income Per Share: Not reported in the filing.

Business Highlights

  • Business focus: Actively seeking a business combination target across fintech, digital assets, healthcare, real estate services, technology and software.
  • Capital formation: Completed IPO on February 6, 2026, raising $115 million into a trust intended to fund a future business combination and related activities.
  • Operational readiness: Sponsor committed to providing pre- and post-IPO funding and advisory support to cover diligence, marketing and transaction costs associated with pursuing a target.
  • Regulatory environment: Noted that new 2024 SPAC rules and pending climate disclosure requirements may increase disclosure needs, timelines and costs when completing a business combination.

Original SEC Filing: Cantor Equity Partners VI, Inc. [ CEPS ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

data.title: Blue Dolphin Energy 10-K: $279.4M Revenue, $(0.38) Loss Per Share
data.description:
Blue Dolphin Energy reported total revenue of $279.4 million for the year, a 12.0% decline from the prior year driven by lower market pricing and reduced refinery sales volumes, and posted a net loss per common share of $(0.38). The company’s net loss narrowed to $5.6 million from $8.6 million a year earlier, reflecting improved refining margins and lower inventory impairment. Gross profit improved to $8.7 million and operating loss narrowed to $(1.5) million on the year.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue from operations: $279.4 million, decreased 12.0% vs 2024 due to lower market pricing and ~11% lower sales volumes
  • Gross profit: $8.7 million, improved from $3.9 million in 2024 driven by more favorable refining margins despite lower volumes
  • Loss from operations: $(1.5) million, improved vs $(4.3) million in 2024
  • Net loss: $(5.6) million, improved from $(8.6) million in 2024
  • Loss per common share (Basic and Diluted): $(0.38), improved from $(0.58) in 2024

Business Highlights

  • Refining margin improvement: Narrower net loss driven by more favorable refining margins despite lower sales volumes.
  • Revenue shift: Total revenue fell ~12% year-over-year due to lower market pricing and approximately 11% lower refinery sales volumes.
  • Operational availability: Nixon refinery downtime improved to 24 days (from 27), which included one 12-day planned turnaround.
  • Midstream stability: Tolling and terminaling EBITDA rose ~7% as storage and toll costs declined despite a modest revenue drop.
  • Emissions and efficiency actions: Upgraded flare gas monitoring to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, optimize combustion, and cut costs.

Original SEC Filing: BLUE DOLPHIN ENERGY CO [ BDCO ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Lululemon convinces US judge to overturn Nike shoe patent verdict
data.description:
By Blake Brittain

A federal judge in Manhattan on Tuesday reversed a jury's verdict that athleticwear maker Lululemon infringed a patent related to the sneaker giant's manufacturing techniques.

U.S. District Judge Arun Subramanian said that Nike's patent at issue in the case was invalid, overturning the jury's $355,450 award against Lululemon.

Spokespeople for the companies did not immediately respond to requests for comment on the decision.

Beaverton, Oregon-based Nike sued Lululemon for patent infringement in 2023. It said that the Canadian company's Chargefeel, Strongfeel and Blissfeel running and training shoes infringed two Nike patents covering innovations in sneaker structures.

Nike requested at least 5% of Lululemon's revenues from the shoes in damages, according to a court filing.

A jury determined last year that Lululemon infringed one of the two patents covering a method for knitting the upper parts of sneakers. Subramanian said on Tuesday that the infringed patent was invalid because it would have been obvious to an ordinary person in the field when it was invented.

Nike separately sued Lululemon in New York in 2022, alleging that Lululemon's Mirror Home Gym infringed other Nike patents. That case is still ongoing.

The shoe patent case is Nike Inc v. Lululemon USA Inc, U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York, No. 1:23-cv-00771.

For Nike: Michael Harris, Christopher Renk, Aaron Bowling and Amy Dewitt of Arnold & Porter Kaye Scholer

For Lululemon: Ali Razai, Jason White and Brandon Smith of Morgan Lewis & Bockius

Read more:

Nike sues Lululemon, says footwear infringes patents

US jury says Lululemon infringed Nike shoe patent

data.title: Nike Stock Slides on Tariff Impact Despite Earnings Beat; Turnaround Continues — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kit Norton

Nike fiscal third-quarter earnings are due after the closing bell Tuesday and management will be seeking to convince investors that the company's business turnaround is the real deal.

Wall Street expects the Beaverton, Ore.-based footwear and athletic apparel retailer to post fiscal third-quarter earnings of 29 cents per share, representing a 46.3% drop-off compared with a year ago, with revenue totaling $11.23 billion, which would mark a 0.3% decline year-over-year, according to FactSet.

A team of Jefferies analysts, led by Randal Konik, on Monday wrote they expect fiscal third-quarter performance to be driven by sales in North America and in Nike's wholesale segment. However, the analysts believe the China market and direct-to-consumer sales "remain under pressure."

The firm added that Nike is progressing in turning around its business and that momentum is building in North America.

The third-quarter earnings results come at 4:15 p.m. Eastern time on Tuesday. Company executives are scheduled to hold the conference call at 5 p.m. ET.

During the conference call, investors are expected to be looking to see if the company changes course and decides to provide full-year guidance. Nike has refrained from offering a full-year outlook for several quarters now as it works on its business turnaround.

Recent investor focus has been on Nike leadership and CEO Elliott Hill, who took the job in October 2024, on improving the company's business direction and on maintaining stable growth.

However, the market has been unconvinced thus far by Hill and Nike.

Nike stock advanced 1.7% to $52.10 on Tuesday ahead of the earnings report. For comparison, the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 1.3% and 0.9%, respectively.

On Monday, Nike closed at $50.98, it's lowest closing price since Oct. 13, 2017, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Entering Tuesday, Nike shares were down 17.59% in March, part of a 19.57% decline on the year.

In December 2025, Nike surpassed Wall Street predictions for both fiscal second-quarter earnings and revenue, but investors were disappointed over declining profits and lackluster sales in China.

Nike's third-quarter guidance also did not sit well with investors, with the footwear and athletic apparel retailer forecasting modest growth in North America with trends in Greater China and Converse in line with the second quarter.

Nike also expects gross margins to be down between 1.75 and 2.25 percentage points from a year ago.

"Over the past several quarters, Nike has struggled significantly, amid a confluence of unfavorable external and internal hindrances, which have weighed meaningfully upon sales growth and profit expansion," Oppenheimer analysts Brian Nagel and Andrew Chasanoff wrote Friday.

The analysts added that challenges persist for Nike but the current share price indicates "downbeat market sentiment," which could lead to "favorable re-rating as fundamental trends at the company re-strengthen and start to consistently top Street forecasts."

Oppenheimer has a price target of $120 on Nike, along with a "top pick" designation in its "consumer growth & ecommerce" coverage group. The firm has an Outperform rating on Nike.

It appears Nike's turnaround is far from over. In a March 5 regulatory filing, Nike said it expects to take a pretax charge of about $300 million, mostly in the third quarter, because of employee severance costs over a nine month period ending Feb. 28. The company said the move comes as management continues to evaluate opportunities to operate more efficiently and profitably.

However, the Jefferies analysts wrote that Nike's business is tracking in the right direction and that the upcoming FIFA World Cup is a clear global catalyst.

"We advise buying shares now as the turnaround advances," Konik wrote. Jefferies has a Buy rating on the stock with a $110 price target.

Write to Kit Norton at kit.norton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: S&P lifts Cook Islands credit rating as tourism boosts finances
data.description:
S&P Global Ratings raised the Cook Islands' long-term sovereign credit rating to BB- from B+ late on Tuesday, citing a tourism-driven economic rebound and fiscal prudence that has strengthened government finances.

It assigned a stable outlook to the rating and affirmed the Pacific island nation's short-term sovereign rating at B and kept its transfer and convertibility assessment at AAA.

S&P said the upgrade reflected "buoyant" economic conditions helped by strong tourist arrivals and government spending coming in below budget, particularly on wages and capital expenditure.

Although S&P expects a small fiscal deficit in the current year, reflecting the withdrawal of budget support from New Zealand and higher capital spending, it said stronger-than-expected tourism income and reprioritised expenditure should cushion the impact in later years.

The agency expects Cook Islands' net debt to continue falling after 2026, remaining around 15% to 20% of GDP over the following three years. Net debt dropped to 16.4% of GDP in fiscal 2025 from a peak of 37.6% in fiscal 2022, aided by rising tourism revenue and concessional donor financing.

Still, the agency said the rating remained constrained by the Cook Islands' narrow economic base, weak statistical capacity, lack of independent monetary policy and exposure to external shocks, including higher fuel costs and strains in relations with New Zealand.
